sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value NUMERIC, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, name) VALUES (1, NULL, 'test1'), (2, 10.5, 'test2'), (3, 20.5, 'test3'), (4, 30.0, 'sample4');
SELECT STDDEV(value) FROM test_data WHERE name SIMILAR TO 'test%';
SELECT id, STDDEV(value) OVER (PARTITION BY name) AS std_dev_value FROM test_data GROUP BY id, name HAVING std_dev_value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY std_dev_value;
SELECT id, value FROM test_data WHERE value > (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_data WHERE name SIMILAR TO 'sample%') AND name SIMILAR TO 'test%';
SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE name SIMILAR TO CONCAT('%', 'test', '%') AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE other_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO other_data (id, description) VALUES (1, 'desc1'), (2, 'desc2'), (3, NULL);
SELECT a.id, a.value, b.description FROM test_data a JOIN other_data b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.description SIMILAR TO 'desc%';
SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL GROUP BY name HAVING AVG(value) > 0;
SELECT STDDEV(value) AS std_dev FROM (SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE name IS NULL) AS subquery WHERE id SIMILAR TO '[1-4]';
